channel.title: INtech
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from INtech, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Here's Why I'd Buy the Last Samsung Soundbar First
item.description: Going one or two generations back in Samsung's Q990 soundbar series is the smartest way to get insane home theater sound.

item.title: The Best Mattress Toppers (2026): Supportive, Plush, Memory Foam
item.description: These toppers will transform your mattress into exactly what you need, whether that’s a super-plush pillow top, memory foam, or targeted back support.

item.title: Fusion startup Helion hits blistering temps as it races toward 2028 deadline
item.description: Helion's Polaris device hit 150 million degrees C recently, a milestone that nudges the company toward its commercial power plant that will sell elect...

item.title: Waymo is asking DoorDash drivers to shut the doors of its self-driving cars
item.description: If a passenger accidentally leaves a Waymo door open, the vehicle can get stuck in place.

item.title: IBM will hire your entry-level talent in the age of AI
item.description: IBM plans to triple its entry-level hiring in the U.S. in 2026, but these jobs will have different tasks than in previous years.

item.title: Waymo Asks the DC Public to Pressure Their City Officials
item.description: Stuck in regulatory limbo, the self-driving-vehicle developer is encouraging residents of Washington, DC, to message public officials to help get its ...

item.title: ‘Uncanny Valley’: ICE’s Secret Expansion Plans, Palantir Workers’ Ethical Concerns, and AI Assistants
item.description: In this episode of Uncanny Valley, our hosts dive into WIRED’s scoop about a secret Trump administration campaign extending right into your backyard.

item.title: Amid disappointing earnings, Pinterest claims it sees more searches than ChatGPT
item.description: Pinterest's stock tumbles after an earnings miss, with higher-than-expected usage its only bright spot.

item.title: For $1M, you can pay Bryan Johnson (or BryanAI?) to teach you how to live longer
item.description: The longevity-obsessed investor Bryan Johnson is charging $1 million to sign up for his "Immortals" program.

item.title: The Asus Zenbook S 16 Is $500 Off and Has Never Been This Cheap
item.description: The Asus Zenbook S 16 was a favorite of ours last year, but the price was always too high. Now it has dropped to $1,000, and it’s a total steal.
